es.example: A la larga, la paciencia rinde.
es.exampleTranslation: In the long run, patience pays off.

it.example: A lungo andare, la pazienza paga.
it.exampleTranslation: In the long run, patience pays.

bridge.note: Italian keeps the verb walking (andare, an infinitive inside the phrase); Spanish freezes it into an adjective-noun (la larga). Same distance, different grammar to get there.

interference.es_to_it: 'A la lunga' exists in Italian too — but 'a lungo andare' is the richer fixed form; note it keeps an INFINITIVE (andare) where Spanish nominalises.
interference.it_to_es: 'A largo andar' is not Spanish. Say a la larga.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm.
